interactions.summary: The host connects Trump to memes and bloodline power; Jiang calls Trump a psychic demon fed by emotion, then explains elite bloodline belief as a justification for rule by inherited divine superiority.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization is consistent enough for this packet: SPEAKER_00 carries host questions, intro, outro, or sponsor reads; SPEAKER_01 carries Jiang answers. Sponsor ad segments are not Jiang claims.

claims.claim: Jiang calls Trump a psychic demon fed by intense love and hate, suggesting attention and emotion empower him regardless of valence.
Jiang says sacred-bloodline belief helps elites explain why they should rule despite encountering smarter people: pure, divine, or heroic blood justifies inherited power.

signature_moments.moment: Trump is a psychic demon who treats hate and love as the same food.
signature_moments.source_phrase: For him, hate, love is the same thing.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the sharpest formulation of the attention model.

signature_moments.moment: Bloodline ideology lets mediocre elites call themselves reincarnated divine beings.
signature_moments.source_phrase: my blood is better. My blood is pure.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains hierarchy as mythic self-protection.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's image for an attention entity strengthened by intense emotions.
A conspiracy framework Jiang says elites use to imagine inherited sacred rule.

chronology_notes.note: The Trump-as-meme and bloodline claims are presented as 2026 speculation, not verified evidence.

uncertainty_notes.note: Several claims in this packet are explicitly speculative or conspiratorial in Jiang's framing; preserve attribution to this interview rather than promoting them as established fact.
